case_title: JANET HALEY VS. HEIDI ANN RICHARDSON et al

ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT HEIDI ANN RICHARDSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT IN PART; DENYING SUMMARY ADJUDICAITON IN PART

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HEIDI RICHARDSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND THE ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION AND GRANTED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF PRESENTED EVIDENCE TO RAISE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER DEFENDANT ACTED OUTRAGEOUSLY AND INTENTIONALLY AND CAUSED PLAINTIFF TO SUFFER ACTUAL AND SEVERE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, DEFENDANT'S STATEMENTS WERE FALSE AND DEFAMATORY ON THEIR FACE, PLAINTIFF SUFFERED SPECIAL DAMAGES, PLAINTIFF HAD AN ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIP WITH THE PROBABILITY OF FUTURE ECONOMIC BENEFIT, AND DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS WERE WRONGFUL. THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS BECAUSE DEFENDANT OWED NO DUTY TO PLAINTIFF AS A MATTER OF LAW. COUNSEL SUMBITS PROPOSED ORDERS TO THE COURT FOR CONSIDERATION. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

CHRISTOPHER GARRETT APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT HEIDI RICHARDSON. THE COURT MADE A PERSONAL DISCLOSURE THAT JUDGE MILLER AND JEFFREY ALLEN ATTENDED KINDERGARTEN TOGETHER. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT HER ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. THERE WAS NO OBJECTION RAISED BY THE DEFENDANT AFTER THE DISCLOSURE WAS MADE. MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-04-2012 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-19-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT HEIDI RICHARDSON'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is off calendar per Notice of Dismissal of Entire Case filed on December 3, 2012. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-30-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: appearances:
mary shea hagebols, esq., shea law offices, 510.208.4422, and nina paul, esq., van de poel, levy & allen, 925.934.6102, for plaintiff janet haley.
chris garrett, esq., guichard, teng & portell, 925.459.8440, for defendant heidi richardson.

ruling: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT HEIDI RICHARDSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT or in the alternative, summary adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first, second, and third causes of action and granted as to the fourth cause of action. Plaintiff presented evidence to raise triable issues whether Defendant acted outrageously and intentionally and caused Plaintiff to suffer actual and severe emotional distress, Defendant's statements were false and defamatory on their face, Plaintiff suffered special damages, Plaintiff had an economic relationship with the probability of future economic benefit, and Defendant's actions were wrongful. The fourth cause of action fails because Defendant owed no duty to Plaintiff as a matter of law. counsel sumbits proposed orders to the court for consideration. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
